examples.title: Algorithm: Lexical Analysis
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the basic steps of lexical analysis
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LexicalAnalysis
INPUT: SourceCode
OUTPUT: TokenStream

BEGIN
 Initialize InputBuffer
 WHILE NOT EndOfFile DO
 ReadNextCharacter
 IF CharacterIsWhitespace THEN
 SkipWhitespace
 ELSE IF CharacterIsComment THEN
 SkipComment
 ELSE
 IdentifyToken
 AddTokenToStream
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the primary steps of lexical analysis: reading input, skipping whitespace and comments, identifying tokens, and adding them to the output stream.

examples.title: Python: Lexical Analysis
examples.description: Python implementation of a basic lexical analyzer
examples.code:
import re

def lexical_analysis(source_code):
 # Define token patterns using regular expressions
 token_patterns = [
 (r'\d+', 'NUMBER'),
 (r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z_0-9]*', 'IDENTIFIER'),
 (r'\+', 'PLUS'),
 (r'\-', 'MINUS'),
 (r'\*', 'MULTIPLY'),
 (r'\/', 'DIVIDE'),
 (r'\(', 'LPAREN'),
 (r'\)', 'RPAREN'),
 ]

 # Initialize the token stream
 token_stream = []

 # Iterate over the source code
 for line in source_code.split('\n'):
 # Remove whitespace and comments
 line = re.sub(r'\s+', '', line)
 line = re.sub(r'#.*', '', line)

 # Identify tokens
 for pattern, token_type in token_patterns:
 for match in re.finditer(pattern, line):
 token_stream.append((token_type, match.group()))

 return token_stream
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates a basic lexical analyzer using regular expressions to define token patterns. It iterates over the source code, removes whitespace and comments, identifies tokens, and adds them to the output stream.
